methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Icarus is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Henry Kwan. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: four at Series A (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, Ares Industries at $150M, and NODA AI at $125M) and one at seed stage (Tenna Systems at $60M). The broader comp universe includes two public companies and eight private firms across defense technology.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields an implied valuation of $150M. This reflects the median across the five-company sample, which spans a range from $60M to $500M. The peer set captures meaningful variation in stage and valuation while maintaining sector and stage consistency.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, anchored on the $150M median. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size (one method, five comps), stage-mixing in the peer set (four Series A versus one seed), and the wide dispersion within the comp range itself ($60M–$500M). Without additional triangulation methods—such as revenue multiples or DCF analysis—confidence in the range remains constrained. Icarus's specific product maturity, customer traction, and capital efficiency relative to named peers would materially inform tighter positioning within this band.
